subject: Geography
title: Desert Cities: Las Vegas
description: Learn Desert Cities: Las Vegas in one minute.

hook.text: Las Vegas uses 832 liters of water per person per day—in the middle of the Mojave Desert.

buildup.text: The city gets 90% of its water from Lake Mead, which is rapidly shrinking.

discovery.text: Vegas recycles nearly all indoor water. Outdoor waste—lawns and pools—is the real problem.

twist.text: The city banned ornamental grass in 2022, ripping up 16 square km of lawn.

climax.text: A city built on excess is learning that the desert always has the final word.

punchline.text: You can build a city in the desert, but you can't build water.

quiz.question: Where does Las Vegas get most of its water?
